nameMeanings.name: Cardinall
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Dante, Mario, Angelo, Sergio, Americo, Corinda, Emilio, Ennio, Gildo, Ilio, Luigi, Massimo, Romeo, Serafino, Sisto.

variants.name: Cardinale
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Sal, Salvatore, Mario, Rocco, Vito, Angelo, Antonio, Cosimo, Salvador, Carlo, Cosmo, Francesca, Gasper, Gennaro, Angelina, Demetrio, Gaspar, Gerardo, Julio, Lucrecia, Romero, Rosario.
Italian: from cardinale ‘cardinal’ (see Cardinal ), a nickname for a haughty individual, or a metonymic occupational name for the servant of a cardinal.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: William Cardinall was born in 1367, in Little Bromley, Essex, England, United Kingdom. He had at least 1 daughter with Lady Lettice Knightley. He died in 1406, in his hometown, at the age of 39.

ko.lifeSummary: William Cardinall(은)는 1367년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 리틀 브롬리에서 태어났다. 그는 Lady Lettice Knightley(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1406년 그의 고향에서 39세의 나이로 사망했다.
